query: AI Omnibus provisional agreement European Parliament Council formal adoption status date

{"query":"AI Omnibus provisional agreement European Parliament Council formal adoption status date","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1648,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://datamatters.sidley.com/2026/06/22/eu-lawmakers-reach-provisional-agreement-to-delay-key-eu-ai-act-obligations/","title":"EU Lawmakers Reach Provisional Agreement to Delay Key EU AI Act Obligations | Data Matters Privacy Blog","content":"On 16 June 2026, the European Parliament voted to adopt the provisional agreement — although, formal adoption remains subject to European Council approval.
The application date for this obligation will be delayed until 2 December 2026, for providers who have already placed their systems on the market before 2 August 2026.
The AI Act requires that certain “high-risk” AI systems are registered in an EU database.
The provisional agreement shifts the obligation imposed on providers and deployers of AI systems to ensure AI literacy to one where the Commission and Member States “support and facilitate” providers / deployers to provision the development of AI literacy.
Confirming that the EU AI Office will supervise AI systems based on general-purpose AI models developed by the same provider, while national authorities retain competence in specific sectors, such as law enforcement, border management, judicial authorities, and financial institutions.
The European Council has indicated that formal adoption is expected “in the coming weeks.” Both co-legislators have also emphasised their intention to complete the process before 2 August 2026, i.e., the date on which the current “high-risk” AI obligations are otherwise scheduled to apply.

- Council agrees position to streamline rules on Artificial Intelligence (press release, 13 March 2026)

tavily.raw: {"query":"AI Omnibus provisional agreement European Parliament Council formal adoption status date","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.gibsondunn.com/eu-ai-act-omnibus-agreement-postponed-high-risk-deadlines-and-other-key-changes","title":"EU AI Act Omnibus Agreement — Postponed High-Risk Deadlines and Other Key Changes - Gibson Dunn","content":"Its obligations roll out in staggered phases, with the most consequential provisions — covering high-risk AI systems (HRAIS) — originally set to apply from 2 August 2026 (for Annex III HRAIS) and 2 August 2027 (for Annex I HRAIS). After an initial round of trilogue negotiations on 28 April ended without success, the institutions returned to the table and reached a provisional political agreement on 6 May, subsequently confirmed by Member State representatives in the Council on 13 May. Formal adoption and publication in the Official Journal are expected in the coming weeks, in advance of the 2 August 2026 deadline. Under the proposal, high-risk AI system obligations under the AI Act will be postponed by over a year compared to the original timeline: stand-alone Annex III systems (covering, among others, recruitment, credit scoring, law enforcement, education, and border control tools) will now need to comply by **2 December 2027**, and AI embedded in regulated products under Annex I — such as medical devices, machinery, and vehicles — by **2 August 2028**.","score":0.82117355,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://hayes-solicitors.ie/news/the-european-parliament-and-council-reach-agreement-on-the-ai-digital-omnibus-regulation-amending-the-ai-act","title":"The European Parliament and Council Reach Agreement on the AI ...","content":"On 7 May 2026, the European Parliament and the Council reached a provisional agreement on the EU's AI Digital Omnibus package.","score":0.8184036,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://datamatters.sidley.com/2026/06/22/eu-lawmakers-reach-provisional-agreement-to-delay-key-eu-ai-act-obligations","title":"EU Lawmakers Reach Provisional Agreement to Delay Key EU AI Act Obligations | Data Matters Privacy Blog","content":"# EU Lawmakers Reach Provisional Agreement to Delay Key EU AI Act Obligations. On 7 May 2026, following extensive negotiations, the European Council and European Parliament reached a provisional agreement on the EU Digital Omnibus on AI (AI Omnibus) which proposes targeted amendments to the EU Artificial Intelligence Act (AI Act). The EU AI Act is the EU’s flagship framework for governing the development, deployment, and use of certain AI systems in the EU. This included the core obligations for certain “high-risk” AI systems. Through this proposal, the Commission aimed to introduce “*simplification measures to ensure timely, smooth, and proportionate implementation of certain of the AI Act’s provisions.*” Central to the proposal was a delay to the implementation timetable for “high-risk” AI systems. The European Parliament indicated in its press release that the intention is to do so before 2 August 2026, i.e., the current implementation date for the AI Act’s “high-risk” AI system rules. Delayed Application of Obligations for “High Risk” AI Systems**.","score":0.8131201,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.europarl.europa.eu/committees/en/agreement-reached-on-updated-ai-rules/product-details/20260608CDT16063","title":"Agreement Reached on Updated AI Rules | Subject files | Home | IMCO | Committees | European Parliament","content":"# Agreement Reached on Updated AI Rules. A hand juggles a circle containing the initials AI and a figurative human head, in profile. The image is sprinkled with iluminated dots. IMCO and LIBE Members adopted by overwhelming majority the provisional agreement resulting from interinstitutional negotiations on the Digital Omnibus on AI. The co-legislators reached the agreement on 7 May 2026, aiming to make it easier for providers to comply with the AI Act while maintaining its main provisions and risk-based approach. A plenary vote is scheduled for the June session. ## Share this page:. ### Committees. ### European Parliament.","score":0.79732054,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.modulos.ai/blog/eu-ai-act-omnibus-deal","title":"EU AI Act Delayed: The Omnibus Deal Closed… | Modulos Blog","content":"# EU AI Act Delayed: The Omnibus Deal Closed on 7 May 2026. Yes, the EU AI Act has been delayed. On 7 May 2026, the Council of the EU and the European Parliament reached provisional political agreement on the Digital Omnibus on AI. High-risk obligations under Annex III now apply from **2 December 2027**, and AI embedded in regulated products under Annex I applies from **2 August 2028**. This is the deal, this is the new compliance horizon, and it is almost certainly the only delay you will get. * **Fixed application dates for high-risk AI systems.** Stand-alone Annex III systems (employment, education, credit, biometrics, critical infrastructure, law enforcement, justice, migration) apply from **2 December 2027**. * **Annex I sectoral compromise.** The machinery regulation is carved out from direct AI Act applicability, with health and safety requirements for high-risk AI added through delegated acts under the machinery regulation itself. | **2 August 2028** | High-risk obligations apply for AI embedded in Annex I regulated products |.","score":0.74363416,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.92,"request_id":"aa392b57-1e75-4e3a-8fbb-a5d51913b0fb"}
